I sleep well every night. I try hard to remember my dreams and the only way that I can is to talk about them with my wife in the morning. I remember this one dream where I was on a bus going somewhere. As I was looking out the window I saw barren land like that a person would see in Ireland or a place like that where there are no houses, trees or even animals.

I felt like I was going somewhere with no destination. I always keep wondering where they destination would be. As I talked about this dream with my wife she always mentions that one cannot interpret a dream literally. Rather it is like a metaphor that represents something else entirely.

My wife mentioned that from the dream I was probably searching for meaning in my life concerning my vocational interests. I am retired now and have no ambitions of going back to work for any reason. But I still search for ways to expand my skillset, such as writing to write short stories. Maybe the bus ride is the vehicle that allows me to learn more of myself than ever before?

DreamMemory:
Most have been utterly banal. Driving somewhere to get food. Doing work on car. Going shopping. Meeting up with people I would normally encounter. 

The most vivid was simply the most recent. I found myself visiting relatives. I was invited to my aunt's hotel room, in a building that most resembled the iconic Book-Cadillac in Detroit, being told "I have the cure here." Presumably I was suffering from some sort of ailment. We are then transported to the beach with my immediate family, walking along for some miles. There were several docks and piers. At each one, I paused, procured a frozen pepperoni pizza, cooked it, and ate a few slices, then caught up with the others. After five or six times, we decided to return to wherever it is we came from and -- for whatever reason -- I was horrified that they would discover the pizzas. I ran ahead and disposed of them in garbage cans along with broken dishes. I am then transported, entirely alone, to some sort of query. There is a soundtrack this time: "Good Morning" by the Dandy Warhols. There is a road travelling through it up ahead, which I would like to reach, but every time I manage to approach it I am met with a chasm below that stops me. Inevitably I look down, and there are large crushing machines vacuuming up crabs and disintegrating them. I despair, and as the song ends, I wake up.
DreamQ_Frequency: Three or more times a week
